tools.name: c64_program
tools.title: Runs PRG/CRT files, uploads BASIC or ASM, and coordinates batch test flows.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for program upload, execution, and batch workflows.

inputSchema.description: Program operations available via the c64_program tool.

oneOf.description: Load a PRG from Ultimate storage without executing it.

path.description: Path to the PRG image. Use an Ultimate-visible filesystem path on c64u, or a host-local filesystem path on VICE.

symbolsFile.description: Path to a VICE symbol file (.vs) to load alongside the PRG. Symbols are used by the disassemble tool (VICE only).

oneOf.description: Load and execute a PRG from Ultimate-visible storage on c64u or a host-local path on VICE.

path.description: Path to the PRG image. Use an Ultimate-visible filesystem path on c64u, or a host-local filesystem path on VICE.

symbolsFile.description: Path to a VICE symbol file (.vs) to load alongside the PRG. Symbols are used by the disassemble tool (VICE only).

oneOf.description: Mount and run a CRT cartridge image.

path.description: Path to the CRT image on Ultimate-visible storage. CRT mounting is only supported on c64u.

oneOf.description: Upload Commodore BASIC v2 source and execute it immediately.

program.description: Commodore BASIC v2 program source to upload and run.

verify.description: Run post-execution polling to ensure the BASIC program executed without errors.

oneOf.description: Assemble 6502/6510 source, upload the PRG, and execute it.

program.description: Assembly source that will be assembled to a PRG and executed.

verify.description: Run post-execution polling to confirm the assembly program remained stable.

oneOf.description: Run multiple PRG/CRT programs with post-run assertions.

continueOnError.description: Continue running programs after assertion failure

durationMs.description: Duration to run each program before assertions

resetDelayMs.description: Delay after reset operations to allow the platform to settle.

oneOf.description: Capture screen, memory, and debug registers into an artifact bundle.
Show a platform-customized greeting on one or more configured backends, capture screenshots, and verify the results.

messageTemplate.description: Greeting template. Use {PLATFORM}/{BACKEND} for uppercase or {platform}/{backend} for lowercase substitution.

verify.description: Poll the text screen and require the rendered greeting to appear.

captureScreenshot.description: Capture a framebuffer screenshot and save it as a PNG per backend.

outputPath.description: Directory for screenshots and the workflow summary JSON.

restoreActiveBackend.description: Restore the backend that was active before the workflow started.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum time to wait for each greeting to appear on screen.

pollIntervalMs.description: Delay between screen polls while verifying the greeting.

_meta.summary: Runs PRG/CRT files, uploads BASIC or ASM, and coordinates batch test flows.

examples.name: Run PRG from storage
examples.description: Load and execute a PRG in one call

examples.name: Upload BASIC source
examples.description: Send inline BASIC to the C64 and run it

examples.name: Show greeting on both backends
examples.description: Render a platform-specific greeting on VICE and C64U with screenshot verification

workflowHints: Choose BASIC or assembly uploaders based on the language you just generated for the user.
Prefer PRG or CRT runners when the user supplies a file path instead of source text; PRG paths are host-local on VICE and Ultimate-visible on c64u.
For a quick visible confirmation on VICE and/or C64U, prefer cross_platform_greeting instead of composing manual backend switches and BASIC upload steps.

tools.name: c64_memory
tools.title: Reads or writes RAM, captures the screen, or waits for text matches in one tool.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for memory I/O, screen reads, and screen polling.

inputSchema.description: Memory operations available via the c64_memory tool.

oneOf.description: Read a range of bytes and return a hex dump with address metadata.

address.description: Start address expressed as $HHHH, decimal, or a documented symbol name.

length.description: Number of bytes to read starting from the resolved address.

oneOf.description: Write a hexadecimal byte sequence into RAM.

address.description: Destination address expressed as $HHHH, decimal, or a documented symbol name.

bytes.description: Hex byte sequence like $AABBCC or AA BB CC to write starting at the resolved address.

verify.description: When true, verify the write by reading before and after the change; Ultimate hardware also pauses during verification.

expected.description: Optional hex data expected before the write (verifies before writing).

mask.description: Optional verification mask (hex); only bits set in the mask are compared.

abortOnMismatch.description: Abort the write when the pre-write verification fails.

oneOf.description: Return the current 40x25 text screen converted to ASCII.
Poll the screen until a substring or regex appears, or timeout elapses.
Disassemble a memory region into annotated 6502/6510 instructions (VICE only).

address.description: Start address as $HHHH, decimal, or symbol name.

length.description: Number of bytes to read and disassemble.

instructionCount.description: Stop after this many instructions (overrides length as a termination condition).

_meta.summary: Reads or writes RAM, captures the screen, or waits for text matches in one tool.

examples.name: Read colour RAM
examples.description: Read 16 bytes starting at $D800

examples.name: Wait for READY prompt
examples.description: Poll until the READY. prompt appears

examples.name: Disassemble entry point
examples.description: Disassemble 20 instructions starting at $0810 (VICE only)

workflowHints: Pair memory operations with documentation snippets so addresses and symbols stay meaningful to the user.
Confirm intent before mutating RAM and explain how the change affects the running program.

tools.name: c64_sound
tools.title: Adjusts SID registers, plays files, composes music, and runs verification captures.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for SID control, playback, composition, and analysis workflows.

inputSchema.description: Sound operations available via the c64_sound tool.

oneOf.description: Capture raw stereo PCM samples from the C64 Ultimate audio UDP stream.

count.description: Number of stereo sample pairs to capture.

encoding.description: Encoding used for the PCM sample payload.

oneOf.description: Set the SID master volume register at $D418 (0-15).

volume.description: Desired SID master volume. Values outside 0-15 are clamped.

oneOf.description: Soft or hard reset of SID registers to clear glitches.

hard.description: If true, perform a hard register scrub; otherwise silence voices softly.

oneOf.description: Trigger a SID voice with configurable waveform, ADSR, and pitch.

note.description: Musical note like A4, C#5, or Bb3. Overrides frequencyHz if provided.

frequencyHz.description: Explicit frequency in Hz. Used when note is omitted.

pulseWidth.description: Pulse width (0-4095). Used for pulse waveform.

oneOf.description: Release a SID voice by clearing its gate bit.
Silence all SID voices with optional audio verification.

verify.description: When true, record audio after silencing to confirm the SID output is quiet.

oneOf.description: Play a SID file stored on the Ultimate filesystem.

path.description: Absolute or Ultimate filesystem path to the SID, PRG, or audio file.

songnr.description: Song number within the SID file (0-based index).

oneOf.description: Play a MOD tracker module via the Ultimate SID player.

path.description: Absolute or Ultimate filesystem path to the SID, PRG, or audio file.

oneOf.description: Generate a lightweight SID arpeggio playback sequence.

root.description: Root note for the arpeggio such as C4 or A#3.

preset.description: Optional musical preset to influence timing/expression.

oneOf.description: Compile SIDWAVE or CPG source and optionally play it immediately.

sidwave.description: SIDWAVE composition represented as YAML/JSON text or an already parsed object.

cpg.description: SIDWAVE composition represented as YAML/JSON text or an already parsed object.

format.description: Source format hint when providing raw text.

dryRun.description: When true, skip playback and only return compilation metadata.

oneOf.description: Compile and play a built-in SID preset such as Für Elise by Beethoven.

preset.description: Named SID preset to compile and play. Canonical public preset: fuer_elise.

verify.description: Capture and analyze audio on supported backends after playback starts.

analysisDurationSeconds.description: Length of the verification recording on analyzable backends.

waitBeforeCaptureMs.description: Delay between starting playback and beginning verification capture.

restoreActiveBackend.description: Restore the backend that was active before the workflow started.

oneOf.description: Compile a SIDWAVE score, play it, and analyze the recording.

sidwave.description: SIDWAVE source in YAML or JSON format.

waitBeforeCaptureMs.description: Delay between starting playback and beginning analysis capture (milliseconds).

expectedSidwave.description: Optional expected SIDWAVE used to refine analysis comparisons.

verifySilenceBefore.description: Run silence verification before playback to ensure a quiet baseline.

verifySilenceAfter.description: Run silence verification after playback to ensure voices are released.

silenceDurationSeconds.description: Audio capture length used for silence verification.

silenceRmsThreshold.description: RMS threshold applied to silence verification captures.

postSilenceWaitMs.description: Delay between main analysis capture and the post-playback silence check (milliseconds).

silenceWaitMs.description: Delay between silencing the SID and recording during pre/post checks (milliseconds).

oneOf.description: Automatically analyze SID playback when verification is requested.

request.description: Natural language user request describing the verification goal.

expectedSidwave.description: SIDWAVE composition represented as YAML/JSON text or an already parsed object.

oneOf.description: Record audio for a fixed duration and return SID analysis metrics.

expectedSidwave.description: SIDWAVE composition represented as YAML/JSON text or an already parsed object.

_meta.summary: Adjusts SID registers, plays files, composes music, and runs verification captures.

examples.name: Capture samples
examples.description: Grab 256 stereo sample pairs from the Ultimate audio stream

examples.name: Trigger SID voice
examples.description: Start voice 1 on C4 with a triangle waveform

examples.name: Play Für Elise
examples.description: Compile and play the built-in Für Elise preset on the active backend

examples.name: Verify silence
examples.description: Stop all voices and confirm the SID output is quiet

workflowHints: Trigger note_on or generate when the user wants immediate SID playback.
Follow up playback changes with analyze or silence verification to provide confident audio feedback.

tools.name: c64_system
tools.title: Manages machine state (pause, reset, power) and schedules recurring background tasks.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for power, reset, menu, and background task control.

inputSchema.description: System operations available via the c64_system tool.

oneOf.description: Pause the machine until resumed.
Resume CPU execution after a pause.
Issue a soft reset without cutting power.
Trigger a firmware reboot to recover from faults.
Request a controlled shutdown via the Ultimate firmware.
Toggle the Ultimate menu button for navigation.
Start a named background task that runs on an interval.

maxIterations.description: Maximum number of iterations (omit for indefinite)

oneOf.description: Stop a specific background task and clear its timer.
Stop every running background task and persist state.
List known background tasks with status metadata.
Summarize diagnostics spans and tool latencies from the current or latest MCP session.

includeTimeline.description: Include the tail of the event timeline.

maxEntries.description: Maximum number of span and timeline entries to return.

_meta.summary: Manages machine state (pause, reset, power) and schedules recurring background tasks.

examples.name: Soft reset
examples.description: Issue a soft reset without cutting power

examples.name: Start background screen capture
examples.description: Launch a recurring read_screen task

examples.name: Inspect performance trace
examples.description: Summarize the current session diagnostics for profiling

workflowHints: Use pause/resume around invasive memory changes, and explain the impact of resets or power changes.
Combine background task operations with list_tasks to monitor long-running diagnostics.

tools.name: c64_select_backend
tools.title: Changes the active backend without restarting the MCP server and reports resulting tool availability.
tools.description: Switch the active backend between C64U hardware and the VICE emulator at runtime.

oneOf.description: Switch the active runtime backend without restarting the MCP server.

backend.description: Backend to activate for subsequent tool calls.

_meta.summary: Changes the active backend without restarting the MCP server and reports resulting tool availability.

examples.name: Switch to VICE
examples.description: Move subsequent tool calls to the emulator backend

workflowHints: Switch backends before invoking backend-specific tools so platform gating stays accurate.

tools.name: c64_graphics
tools.title: Captures frames, renders PETSCII text or art, previews sprites, and displays bitmap images from one tool.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for frame capture and graphics rendering workflows.

inputSchema.description: Graphics operations available via the c64_graphics tool.

oneOf.description: Capture one or more complete video frames from the active backend.

includePixels.description: Include pixel payload bytes in the response.

encoding.description: Encoding used for the pixel payload when included.

oneOf.description: Create PETSCII art from prompts, text, or explicit bitmap data, and optionally display it on the C64.

prompt.description: Natural language prompt describing the desired PETSCII art.

text.description: Exact text to render in PETSCII (overrides prompt derivation).

dryRun.description: When true, skip uploading the generated BASIC program to the C64.

bitmap.description: Explicit bitmap definition for PETSCII rendering.

oneOf.description: Display PETSCII text with optional border and background colours.

text.description: The PETSCII text to print after clearing the screen.

oneOf.description: Display supplied 63-byte sprite data at the requested position and colour by writing memory and patching VIC-II registers directly.

sprite.description: 63-byte sprite definition provided as base64/hex string or array of bytes.

index.description: Sprite index (0-7) to configure in screen memory.

multicolour.description: Enable multicolour mode for the sprite.

oneOf.description: Import an image file, convert it to VIC-II bitmap memory, write it into RAM, and display it.

imagePath.description: Filesystem path of the source image (PNG, JPEG, BMP, GIF, TIFF, and other Jimp-supported formats).

format.description: Bitmap encoding mode to generate for the VIC-II display.

bitmapAddress.description: Absolute C64 memory address, provided as an integer or hex string such as $2000.

screenAddress.description: Absolute C64 memory address, provided as an integer or hex string such as $2000.

backgroundColor.description: Background colour index (0-15). Used for multicolor mode and aspect-ratio padding.

preserveAspect.description: Preserve the source image aspect ratio and pad with the background colour.

oneOf.description: Read VIC-II and CIA2 registers to determine the current graphics mode and memory layout (VICE only).

_meta.summary: Captures frames, renders PETSCII text or art, previews sprites, and displays bitmap images from one tool.

examples.name: Capture one frame
examples.description: Grab the current framebuffer from the active backend

examples.name: Create PETSCII art (dry run)
examples.description: Synthesize art without uploading to the C64

examples.name: Render PETSCII text
examples.description: Print HELLO with blue border

examples.name: Display sprite
examples.description: Show sprite data at coordinates

examples.name: Import bitmap image
examples.description: Convert a PNG into VIC bitmap memory and display it

examples.name: Read VIC-II state
examples.description: Decode graphics mode, memory layout, and colours from VIC-II registers (VICE only)

workflowHints: Use render helpers for PETSCII text, PETSCII art, sprites, and bitmaps; clarify whether PETSCII art executed or stayed a dry run.
Mention sprite positions/colours so follow-up memory inspection stays grounded.

tools.name: c64_rag
tools.title: Returns curated knowledge references for BASIC or 6502/6510 assembly queries.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for BASIC and assembly RAG lookups.

inputSchema.description: RAG operations available via the c64_rag tool.

oneOf.description: Retrieve BASIC references and snippets from the local knowledge base.

q.description: Natural-language query describing the desired BASIC or assembly guidance.

k.description: Maximum number of references to return (1-12).

oneOf.description: Retrieve 6502/6510 assembly references from the local knowledge base.

q.description: Natural-language query describing the desired BASIC or assembly guidance.

k.description: Maximum number of references to return (1-12).

_meta.summary: Returns curated knowledge references for BASIC or 6502/6510 assembly queries.

examples.name: Retrieve BASIC snippet
examples.description: Search for device 4 printing guidance

examples.name: Retrieve assembly snippet
examples.description: Search for raster IRQ examples

workflowHints: Use BASIC retrieval before synthesising new BASIC code and mention primary resources in responses.
For assembly, note registers or addresses surfaced so the user can inspect them further.

tools.name: c64_disk
tools.title: Mount or unmount images, create new disks, list drives, and find programs from one tool.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for disk mounts, listings, image creation, and program discovery.

inputSchema.description: Disk operations available via the c64_disk tool.

oneOf.description: List Ultimate drive slots and their mounted images.
Mount a disk image with optional verification and retries.

image.description: Absolute or Ultimate filesystem path to the disk image.

type.description: Override detected image type when firmware guesses incorrectly.

attachmentMode.description: Attachment mode controlling how the firmware treats the mounted image.

driveMode.description: Drive emulation mode to switch to during verification.

verify.description: When true, power on/reset/verify using the reliability workflow.

powerOnIfNeeded.description: Power on the drive automatically before mounting when verify=true.

resetAfterMount.description: Issue a drive reset after mounting when verify=true.

maxRetries.description: Maximum number of mount retries when verify=true.

retryDelayMs.description: Delay between mount retry attempts when verify=true.

oneOf.description: Remove the mounted image from an Ultimate drive slot.

drive.description: Drive identifier such as drive8, drive9, or unit name defined in the Ultimate UI.

oneOf.description: Inspect metadata for a file on the Ultimate filesystem.

path.description: Absolute or Ultimate filesystem path to the disk image to mount or manage.

oneOf.description: Create a blank disk image of the specified format.

path.description: Destination path on the Ultimate filesystem.

diskname.description: Optional disk label (1-16 characters, converted to PETSCII).

tracks.description: Track count (D64 supports 35 or 40; DNP requires explicit tracks).

oneOf.description: Search for a PRG/CRT by name substring and run the first match.

waitMs.description: Delay after firmware run before returning

captureCandidates.description: Include up to this many candidates in result

_meta.summary: Mount or unmount images, create new disks, list drives, and find programs from one tool.

examples.name: Mount image with verification
examples.description: Power on drive8, mount image, and verify

examples.name: Create D81
examples.description: Create blank D81 image

examples.name: List drives
examples.description: Fetch drive status

workflowHints: Summarise drive state before and after mounts so the user can confirm hardware changes.
Call out when verification retries succeed or fail so follow-up actions are clear.

tools.name: c64_drive
tools.title: Power cycle drive slots, reset IEC state, switch emulation modes, or load custom ROMs.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for drive power, mode, reset, and ROM operations.

inputSchema.description: Drive operations available via the c64_drive tool.

oneOf.description: Issue an IEC reset for the selected drive slot.

drive.description: Drive identifier such as drive8, drive9, or unit name defined in the Ultimate UI.

oneOf.description: Power on a specific Ultimate drive slot.

drive.description: Drive identifier such as drive8, drive9, or unit name defined in the Ultimate UI.

oneOf.description: Power off a specific Ultimate drive slot.

drive.description: Drive identifier such as drive8, drive9, or unit name defined in the Ultimate UI.

oneOf.description: Temporarily load a custom ROM into an Ultimate drive slot.

drive.description: Drive identifier such as drive8, drive9, or unit name defined in the Ultimate UI.

path.description: Absolute or Ultimate filesystem path to the disk image to mount or manage.

oneOf.description: Set the emulation mode for a drive slot (1541/1571/1581).

drive.description: Drive identifier such as drive8, drive9, or unit name defined in the Ultimate UI.

_meta.summary: Power cycle drive slots, reset IEC state, switch emulation modes, or load custom ROMs.

examples.name: Power on drive
examples.description: Enable drive8

examples.name: Set 1581 mode
examples.description: Switch emulation mode

workflowHints: State the resulting power/mode/ROM so the user can reconcile IEC behaviour.
Suggest running c64_disk (op list_drives) to confirm status when appropriate.

tools.name: c64_printer
tools.title: Print text or bitmaps and define custom characters for Commodore or Epson printers.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for Commodore and Epson printing helpers.

inputSchema.description: Printer operations available via the c64_printer tool.

oneOf.description: Generate BASIC that prints text to device 4.

text.description: Text to print on the connected printer.

target.description: Printer target: Commodore MPS (PETSCII) or Epson FX (ESC/P).

secondaryAddress.description: Secondary address for device 4 (0 = upper/graphics, 7 = lowercase).

formFeed.description: Emit a form-feed (CHR$(12)) at the end of the job.

oneOf.description: Print a bitmap row via Commodore (BIM) or Epson ESC/P workflows.

repeats.description: Number of times to repeat the row (1-255).

useSubRepeat.description: Repeat the next byte this many times (Commodore BIM SUB).

ensureMsb.description: Ensure MSB set for Commodore printers.

density.description: Density parameter when using Epson mode '*'.

timesPerLine.description: Number of times to print the row per line (1-10).

oneOf.description: Define custom printer characters (Commodore DLL mode).

firstChar.description: PETSCII code of first character to redefine (33-126).

items.description: Commodore MPS DLL character definition (11 columns).

secondaryAddress.description: Secondary address for device 4 (0 = upper/graphics, 7 = lowercase).

_meta.summary: Print text or bitmaps and define custom characters for Commodore or Epson printers.

examples.name: Print text
examples.description: Generate BASIC for device 4

examples.name: Print bitmap
examples.description: Send Epson graphics row

workflowHints: Mention device/secondary addresses so the user knows which printer workflow ran.
When defining characters, remind the user to send the BASIC program returned in the payload.

tools.name: c64_config
tools.title: List or update settings, access firmware info, take snapshots, and run program shuffle workflows.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for configuration reads/writes, diagnostics, and snapshots.

inputSchema.description: Configuration operations available via the c64_config tool.

oneOf.description: List configuration categories reported by the firmware.
Read a configuration category or specific item.

category.description: Configuration category reported by the Ultimate firmware (e.g. 'Audio').

item.description: Specific configuration item inside the selected category (e.g. 'Volume').

oneOf.description: Write a configuration value in the selected category.

category.description: Configuration category reported by the Ultimate firmware (e.g. 'Audio').

item.description: Configuration item name inside the category.

value.description: Primitive configuration value (string, number, or boolean).

oneOf.description: Apply multiple configuration updates in a single request.
Load configuration from flash storage.
Persist the current configuration to flash storage.
Reset firmware configuration to factory defaults.
Read the Ultimate debug register ($D7FF).
Write a hex value to the Ultimate debug register ($D7FF).

value.description: Hex value (00-FF) to write to the debug register.

oneOf.description: Retrieve Ultimate hardware information and status.
Fetch firmware version details.
Snapshot configuration to disk for later restore or diff.

path.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path where the snapshot file will be written.

oneOf.description: Restore configuration from a snapshot file.

path.description: Snapshot file to read (must contain categories payload).

applyToFlash.description: When true, save the restored configuration to flash immediately.

oneOf.description: Compare the current configuration with a snapshot.
Discover PRG/CRT files and run each with optional screen capture.

root.description: Root path to search for programs

durationMs.description: Duration to run each program in milliseconds

maxPrograms.description: Maximum number of programs to run

outputPath.description: Output directory for run logs and captures

resetDelayMs.description: Delay after reset operations to allow the platform to settle.

_meta.summary: List or update settings, access firmware info, take snapshots, and run program shuffle workflows.

examples.name: List categories
examples.description: Enumerate configuration categories

examples.name: Set volume
examples.description: Adjust audio volume to 70

examples.name: Snapshot config
examples.description: Write configuration snapshot to disk

workflowHints: List categories before changing values so users can confirm firmware-provided names.
Mention when operations persist to flash or modify debug registers to highlight impacts.
Call out snapshot file paths so users can version-control or reuse them later.

tools.name: c64_extract
tools.title: Export sprites or charsets, dump RAM, compute filesystem stats, or run firmware health checks.

stride.description: Stride between sprite candidates in bytes

minSetBits.description: Minimum number of set bits required

pauseDuringRead.description: Pause the machine during memory read

address.description: Start address to scan ($HHHH or decimal). If not provided, scans common locations.

scanRange.description: Range to scan if address not provided: 'common' or 'full'

outputPath.description: File path to write charset binary

pauseDuringRead.description: Pause the machine during memory read

oneOf.description: Dump a RAM range to hex or binary files with manifest metadata.
Walk the filesystem and aggregate counts/bytes by extension.

includeContainers.description: Include container images (d64/d81/etc.) in extension stats

maxSamplesPerExtension.description: Number of sample paths to keep per extension

oneOf.description: Run firmware readiness checks and report status metrics.

Summarise output file paths or sample counts so users can inspect artifacts quickly.

tools.name: c64_stream
tools.title: Start or stop audio/video/debug streaming in a single tool.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for starting and stopping Ultimate streaming sessions.

inputSchema.description: Streaming operations available via the c64_stream tool.

oneOf.description: Start an Ultimate streaming session toward a host:port target.

target.description: Destination host:port or UDP target understood by the firmware.

tools.name: c64_debug
tools.title: Manage breakpoints, inspect registers, and step through code on the VICE backend.
tools.description: Grouped entry point for VICE debugger operations (breakpoints, registers, stepping).

oneOf.description: List all active VICE checkpoints (breakpoints).
Fetch a single checkpoint by id.
Create a new checkpoint (breakpoint) in VICE.

enabled.description: Whether the checkpoint is enabled (default true).

oneOf.description: Remove a checkpoint by id.
Enable or disable a checkpoint by id.

enabled.description: Set to true to enable, false to disable.

oneOf.description: Attach a conditional expression to a checkpoint.
List available registers (metadata).
Read register values, optionally filtered by name or id.

items.description: Selects a register by id or name.

oneOf.description: Write register values.
Single-step CPU execution.
Continue execution until the current routine returns.
Read CPU registers and return the current monitor state.
Poll CPU registers until PC equals expectedPC or timeout elapses.

expectedPC.description: Target PC address to wait for (e.g. $0810).

oneOf.description: Kill and restart the VICE process (managed instances only).

_meta.summary: Manage breakpoints, inspect registers, and step through code on the VICE backend.

examples.name: List checkpoints
examples.description: Show all breakpoints defined in the emulator.

examples.name: Step over instruction
examples.description: Advance one instruction without entering subroutines.

examples.name: Inspect registers
examples.description: Read CPU registers from memspace 0 (default).

examples.name: Create labelled breakpoint
examples.description: Set a breakpoint at $0810 with a descriptive label.

examples.name: Get monitor state
examples.description: Read current CPU state including all registers and PC.

examples.name: Wait for PC
examples.description: Poll until PC reaches $0810 or 5 s elapses.

examples.name: Nuclear reset
examples.description: Kill and restart the VICE process, clearing all state.

workflowHints: Use debugger tools after pausing execution so memory and screen reads remain stable.
Communicate breakpoint effects clearly and restore original state when toggling them.

tools.name: c64_vice

inputSchema.description: VICE emulator operations available via the c64_vice tool.

oneOf.description: Read a VICE configuration resource (safe prefixes only).
Write a VICE configuration resource (safe prefixes only).

tools.name: c64_input
tools.title: Types text, taps keys, and simulates joystick movements in VICE.
tools.description: VICE-only keyboard feed and joystick simulation via CIA1 register writes.

oneOf.description: Send a text string to the keyboard buffer, with PETSCII token expansion.

text.description: Text to type. Use {RETURN}, {CLR}, {F1}…{F8}, {UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT}, colour names, etc.

oneOf.description: Tap a single key or hold it for a duration.

key.description: Key to press: a printable character, or a PETSCII token name (RETURN, F1…F8, UP, etc.).

durationMs.description: How long to hold the key before releasing (default 0 = instant tap).

count.description: Number of times to press the key (default 1).

oneOf.description: Simulate joystick input by writing directly to CIA1 Port A/B registers.

controls.description: List of controls to activate simultaneously.

action.description: press = hold until a release call; release = restore all bits; tap = press then release after durationMs.

durationMs.description: Duration to hold for tap action in ms (default 80).

_meta.summary: Types text, taps keys, and simulates joystick movements in VICE.

examples.name: Type BASIC line
examples.description: Enter a BASIC line and press RETURN

examples.name: Press F1
examples.description: Send the F1 function key

examples.name: Tap joystick right
examples.description: Brief rightward tap on joystick port 2

examples.name: Press fire on port 1
examples.description: Hold fire button on joystick port 1

workflowHints: Use write_text with {RETURN} tokens to automate BASIC entry; prefer key for individual control keys.
Joystick tap is suitable for one-shot moves; use press/release pairs for timed holds.
